page: Vultr voc-g-1c-4gb-30s-amd is an entry-level instance within the Optimized Cloud Compute family. It features a single shared AMD EPYC vCPU operating at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, paired with 4.0 GB of system memory. Storage is provided via a local 30 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. The CPU supports AVX, AES, and SHA_NI instruction sets, though it lacks AVX-512 and AMX capabilities.

Benchmark data reveals a clear contrast between single-core compute capabilities and overall system throughput. The instance achieves a strong single-core CPU performance score of 3035.28 in stress-ng, placing it in the top 25%. However, due to its single-core configuration, multi-core performance is weak. Memory bandwidth is limited, with cached small-block reads at 142.91 GB/sec and uncached large-block writes at 22.58 GB/sec. Single-threaded compression tasks perform at average levels, with gzip compression reaching 28.4 GB/sec.

Given the shared CPU allocation and low memory bandwidth, this server exhibits poor performance in database operations and static web serving throughput. It is highly cost-efficient for lightweight workloads that do not require sustained multi-core execution or high memory throughput. Appropriate workloads include basic single-threaded applications, lightweight utility scripting, development environments, and small-scale text processing tasks.
description: Vultr voc-g-1c-4gb-30s-amd is a shared-CPU instance in the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring 1 AMD EPYC vCPU running at 2.0 GHz, 4.0 GB of RAM, and a 30 GB NVMe SSD. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it supports AVX, AES, and SHA_NI instructions. In benchmarks, the instance demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance with a stress-ng score of 3035.28, though its multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain in the lower percentiles. Database operations and static web serving throughput are limited due to the single-core, shared-resource design. It includes one complimentary public IPv4 address to facilitate basic external connectivity. This server offers a budget-friendly profile for lightweight, single-threaded workloads, utility scripts, and basic development environments that do not require high memory throughput or dedicated CPU resources.
og_description: Vultr voc-g-1c-4gb-30s-amd is a shared-CPU instance featuring 1 AMD EPYC vCPU, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 30 GB of NVMe SSD storage, designed for lightweight single-threaded workloads and utility tasks.
meta_description: Vultr voc-g-1c-4gb-30s-amd features 1 AMD EPYC vCPU, 4.0 GB RAM, and 30 GB NVMe SSD storage, offering a budget-friendly option for basic workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly, shared-CPU instance with local NVMe storage, delivering strong single-core performance for lightweight utility tasks and development environments.
bullet_points: 1 shared AMD EPYC vCPU at 2.0 GHz and 4.0 GB RAM
30 GB local NVMe SSD storage and 1 public IPv4 address
Strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Hardware-accelerated AES, SHA_NI, and AVX instruction support
Best suited for lightweight utility scripts and development environments
